title: The two-cell P0 projection is the best constant approximation

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The residual error from the two-cell P0 projection is no larger than the squared error to any constant value c.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real cell values
c is any real constant comparison value
the P0 projection is the two-cell average on both cells
informal.expected_conclusion: The projection residual energy is bounded by the error to any constant field.

declarations.name: p0ProjectionResidualSq2
declarations.statement: def p0ProjectionResidualSq2 (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := (u0 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) ^ 2 + (u1 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) ^ 2

declarations.name: constApproxError2
declarations.statement: def constApproxError2 (u0 u1 c : Real) : Real := (u0 - c) ^ 2 + (u1 - c) ^ 2

declarations.name: p0Projection2_best_const_approx
declarations.statement: theorem p0Projection2_best_const_approx (u0 u1 c : Real) : p0ProjectionResidualSq2 u0 u1 <= constApproxError2 u0 u1 c

tasks.input: Translate the best constant approximation property of the two-cell P0 projection into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem p0Projection2_best_const_approx (u0 u1 c : Real) : p0ProjectionResidualSq2 u0 u1 <= constApproxError2 u0 u1 c

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by decomposing the error to c into projection residual plus nonnegative constant-projection error.
tasks.target: Prove the error decomposition by ring, prove the remaining constant error nonnegative by sq_nonneg, and finish with linarith.

tasks.input: Find the Mathlib-style nonnegativity fact needed for the squared constant-projection error.
